title: humularia rosea
intro: Humularia rosea, also known as Sabadilla or Schoenocaulon officinale in some regions, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to repel insects and its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Humularia rosea include relief from itching and skin conditions, as well as its potential in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Humularia rosea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the roots and leaves of the plant and then mixing them with a solvent to extract its active compounds.
